title.purpose: Masthead / archival-record header. Serif, balanced wrap.

reading.purpose: The document column itself — serif, 1.8 line-height, capped at 68ch.

body.purpose: UI copy, descriptions, control labels. System sans.

eyebrow.purpose: Masthead eyebrow, dateline, detail keys. Mono, uppercase, 0.16em tracking.

data.purpose: Percentages, counts, revision ids. Mono + tabular-nums so figures never reflow.

shadows.name: resting-card
shadows.purpose: Panels and cards — barely-there separation of leaf-white from the paper.

shadows.name: lifted-leaf
shadows.purpose: The manuscript leaf: a long, soft, low-opacity drop — a sheet held off the desk.

shadows.name: floating-tip
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus tooltips and the contributor popover.

shadows.name: pinned-panel
shadows.purpose: The clicked seal-detail panel — the most-lifted surface in the system.

shadows.name: carved-channel
shadows.purpose: The timeline track, recessed so accumulating ink reads as filling a groove.

shadows.name: pressed-seal
shadows.purpose: Timeline markers and legend seals — a small stamp proud of the track.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default for color/background state changes on buttons and controls.

motion.name: transport-transition
motion.purpose: The timeline fill width and playhead position as the frame advances.

motion.name: tooltip-fade
motion.purpose: Reading-column affordance tooltips fade in instantly on hover.

motion.name: caret-blink
motion.purpose: The writing nib's soft blink; frozen (not removed) under reduced-motion.

motion.name: indeterminate-slide
motion.purpose: The discovering-phase sweep; degrades to a full-track opacity pulse under reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single filled action per view — revision indigo, white text. Used for the main affordance (e.g. Replay revisions).
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary" type="button"><svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 5v14l11-7z"/></svg>Replay revisions</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:0.375rem; border:none; border-radius:0.375rem; padding:0.375rem 0.75rem; font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1.25rem; color:#fff; background:oklch(54% 0.13 264); cursor:pointer; transition:background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:oklch(62% 0.11 264); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:none; box-shadow:0 0 0 2px oklch(98.5% 0.001 106), 0 0 0 4px oklch(70% 0.10 264); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The default non-primary action — leaf-white surface, hairline graphite border, ink text.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:0.375rem; border:1px solid oklch(86.9% 0.005 56); border-radius:0.375rem; padding:0.375rem 0.75rem; font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1.25rem; color:oklch(26.8% 0.007 34); background:#fff; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background:oklch(97% 0.001 106); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline:none; box-shadow:0 0 0 2px oklch(98.5% 0.001 106), 0 0 0 4px oklch(70% 0.10 264); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis / icon-adjacent action (back, dismiss) — transparent, graphite-muted text, soft stone wash on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:0.375rem; border:none; border-radius:0.375rem; padding:0.375rem 0.75rem; font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1.25rem; color:oklch(37.4% 0.01 67); background:transparent; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background:oklch(92.3% 0.003 49 / 0.7); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline:none; box-shadow:0 0 0 2px oklch(98.5% 0.001 106), 0 0 0 4px oklch(70% 0.10 264); }

components.name: Card / Panel
components.description: A resting surface on the paper — leaf-white, hairline border, barely-there shadow. Eyebrow + title + body.
components.html:
Revision 482

Draft converges

Three contributors, 1,204 edits across 6 sessions — reconstructed entirely on this device.
components.css: .ds-card { border-radius:0.5rem; border:1px solid oklch(92.3% 0.003 49); background:#fff; padding:1rem; box-shadow:0 1px 2px oklch(0% 0 0 / 0.05); max-width:22rem; } .ds-eyebrow { margin:0 0 0.5rem; font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,monospace; font-size:0.6875rem; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.16em; color:oklch(55.3% 0.013 58); } .ds-card-title { margin:0 0 0.375rem; font-family:ui-serif,Georgia,Cambria,serif; font-size:1.25rem; font-weight:600; letter-spacing:-0.025em; color:oklch(21.6% 0.006 56); } .ds-card-body { margin:0; font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; line-height:1.5; color:oklch(44.4% 0.011 73); }

components.name: Revision Timeline
components.description: The signature writing-activity stratum: a carved channel filled with an oklch ink-ramp, a standing-nib playhead, and marginalia seals you press to jump through time.
components.html: §⌄
components.css: .ds-tl { padding:1.25rem 0.5rem; max-width:30rem; } .ds-tl-track { position:relative; height:0.625rem; width:100%; border-radius:9999px; background:oklch(92.3% 0.003 49); box-shadow:inset 0 1px 2px oklch(0% 0 0 / 0.07); } .ds-tl-fill { position:absolute; inset:0 38% 0 0; border-radius:9999px; background:linear-gradient(to right, oklch(70% 0.10 264), oklch(62% 0.11 264), oklch(54% 0.13 264)); } .ds-tl-thumb { position:absolute; top:50%; height:1.5rem; width:3px; transform:translate(-50%,-50%); border-radius:9999px; background:oklch(54% 0.13 264); box-shadow:0 1px 3px oklch(54% 0.13 264 / 0.45); outline:2px solid #fff; } .ds-tl-seal { position:absolute; top:50%; display:grid; place-items:center; width:1rem; height:1rem; transform:translate(-50%,-50%); border-radius:9999px; border:1px solid oklch(54% 0.13 264); background:#fff; color:oklch(54% 0.13 264); font-size:12px; line-height:1; box-shadow:0 1px 2px oklch(0% 0 0 / 0.18); cursor:pointer; transition:transform 0.15s ease; } .ds-tl-seal:hover { transform:translate(-50%,-50%) scale(1.1); } .ds-tl-seal-strike { border-color:oklch(55% 0.16 35); color:oklch(55% 0.16 35); }

components.name: Reconstruction Runs
components.description: How the document column renders revision state: accepted text, suggested insert (green + dotted underline + sr-only label), marked-for-deletion (vermilion + strike), and an opaque-structure chip. Color is never the sole signal.
components.html: The committee approved the revised draft proposal ▤ table before recess.
components.css: .ds-reading { max-width:42rem; margin:0; font-family:ui-serif,Georgia,Cambria,serif; font-size:1.0625rem; line-height:1.8; color:oklch(21.6% 0.006 56); } .ds-doc-suggest { border-radius:0.125rem; background:oklch(95% 0.03 165); color:oklch(52% 0.12 165); text-decoration:underline dotted oklch(52% 0.12 165); text-underline-offset:2px; } .ds-doc-strike { border-radius:0.125rem; background:oklch(95% 0.03 35); color:oklch(55% 0.16 35); text-decoration:line-through; text-decoration-color:oklch(55% 0.16 35); } .ds-doc-opaque { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.25rem; margin:0 0.125rem; border-radius:0.25rem; border:1px solid oklch(86.9% 0.005 56); background:oklch(97% 0.001 106); padding:0.125rem 0.375rem; font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; color:oklch(44.4% 0.011 73); vertical-align:baseline; }

components.name: Contributor Chip
components.description: An interactive contributor chip with the collaborator's Google-assigned color as a small ringed dot; hover/focus reveals a content-free detail card.
components.css: .ds-author-chip { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.375rem; border:1px solid oklch(86.9% 0.005 56); border-radius:0.25rem; background:transparent; padding:0.125rem 0.5rem; font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; color:oklch(44.4% 0.011 73); cursor:pointer; transition:background-color 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-author-chip:hover { background:oklch(97% 0.001 106); color:oklch(26.8% 0.007 34); } .ds-author-chip:focus-visible { outline:none; box-shadow:0 0 0 2px oklch(70% 0.10 264); } .ds-author-dot { width:0.5rem; height:0.5rem; flex-shrink:0; border-radius:9999px; border:1px solid oklch(0% 0 0 / 0.1); }

components.name: Privacy Banner
components.description: A collapsed-by-default disclosure on a calm stone surface — orientation, not a hazard. No alarming hue or colored side-stripe; the chevron rotates on open as the non-color affordance.
components.html:
What am I looking at?
DocRewind reconstructed this history on your machine. Nothing was uploaded, and no account was used.
components.css: .ds-banner { overflow:hidden; border-radius:0.375rem; border:1px solid oklch(92.3% 0.003 49); background:oklch(98.5% 0.001 106); font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; max-width:26rem; } .ds-banner-summary { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem; list-style:none; cursor:pointer; padding:0.5rem 0.75rem; font-weight:500; color:oklch(44.4% 0.011 73); transition:color 0.15s ease; } .ds-banner-summary::-webkit-details-marker { display:none; } .ds-banner-summary:hover { color:oklch(21.6% 0.006 56); } .ds-banner-chevron { flex-shrink:0; color:oklch(70.9% 0.01 56); transition:transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-banner[open] .ds-banner-chevron { transform:rotate(90deg); } .ds-banner-body { padding:0.125rem 0.75rem 0.75rem; line-height:1.5; color:oklch(44.4% 0.011 73); }

narrative.overview: DocRewind looks like an archival sheet under raking light, with a working margin running down its spine. The page is cool, near-neutral paper; the document sits on an elevated leaf with a ruled binding margin; and the revision timeline reads as marginalia — small paper seals pressed into the margin of the record, each a mark you can press to jump through time. The personality is approachable, calm, and trustworthy: an everyday utility, not a specialist instrument. The archival calm is in service of approachability — it keeps the chrome quiet so the document and its evolution stay center stage. The tool is meant to disappear into the reading.
keyCharacteristics: Cool archival paper (near-neutral stone), not warm cream — and a full first-class dark theme.
Serif for the document and titles; system sans for UI; mono for data, eyebrows, and revision ids.
One accent — revision indigo — for 'now,' primary actions, and focus, never decoration.
Meaning is never carried by color alone; every state pairs a hue with a glyph, underline, strike, or label.
The manuscript leaf and floating cards lift; everything else stays tonal and flat.
Motion conveys state only and fully degrades under reduced-motion.

rules.name: The One-Indigo Rule
rules.body: Revision indigo means 'now' and 'primary action' — nothing else. It is never used to decorate, fill a background, or color a heading. Its scarcity is what makes the playhead and the active control instantly findable.

rules.name: The Cool-Paper Rule
rules.body: The page is cool, near-neutral stone. Warming it toward cream, sand, ivory, or beige is forbidden — that warm near-white is the generic-AI tell this system was built to avoid.

rules.name: The Never-Color-Alone Rule
rules.body: Every meaning-bearing hue (indigo, green, vermilion, amber) is always paired with a non-color affordance — a glyph, underline, strike, ringed seal, or sr-only label — so meaning survives grayscale and color-vision differences.

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Serif = the record. Sans = the controls. Mono = the figures. A label in serif, body copy in mono, or a revision id in sans is wrong. Keep each voice in its lane.

rules.name: The Honest-Figures Rule
rules.body: Every number that changes (percent, count, revision id, timestamp) is mono + tabular-nums, so it holds its column and never jitters as it climbs.

rules.name: The Earned-Lift Rule
rules.body: A drop shadow means 'this floats above the page.' Only the leaf and genuinely floating overlays (tips, popovers, pinned panels) earn one. Cards and panels stay tonal.

dos: Do keep the page cool, near-neutral paper (cool-paper oklch(98.5% 0.001 106)). Carry any warmth through accent and type, never the body background.
Do reserve revision indigo for 'now,' the single primary action, and focus rings. One filled button per view.
Do pair every meaning-bearing color with a non-color affordance — glyph, underline, strike, ringed seal, or sr-only label. Test every state in grayscale.
Do keep the three type voices in their lanes: serif for the document and titles, system sans for UI, mono + tabular-nums for every figure.
Do spend drop shadows only on the leaf and genuinely floating overlays; let tonal stone layering carry the rest.
Do ship every interactive element with default, hover, focus-visible, active, and disabled states, and a visible 2px revision-indigo-ring focus ring.
Do provide a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation (freeze the caret, pulse the indeterminate bar, drop transitions).
Do prefer native form controls and standard affordances; the tool should feel familiar and recede.
donts: Don't drift the paper toward cream, sand, ivory, or beige — that warm AI-default near-white is the exact aesthetic this system rejects.
Don't build a surveillance / analytics dashboard look: no KPI tiles, no charts-as-decoration, no telemetry sheen.
Don't import flashy consumer-SaaS patterns: no gradient hero banners, no hero-metric template, no marketing polish.
Don't let reconstruction states read as accusatory. Show process neutrally; never score, flag, or editorialize.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text) anywhere. The only sanctioned gradients are the timeline's oklch ink-ramp and the near-flat paper wash.
Don't use a colored side-stripe (border-left/right > 1px) on cards, banners, or alerts. Full hairline borders or tonal tints only.
Don't use glassmorphism / decorative blur, or reach for a heavy shadow to fake hierarchy that tonal layering should carry.
Don't let a second accent compete with revision indigo, or use any meaning-bearing hue as decoration.
